$schema: Taxonomy of controlled values for brief frontmatter. Each axis is independent. Values should be applied only when the brief substantively engages with the topic — not merely mentions it in passing.

values.supportive: Sees clear value, wants to build/use it
values.conditional: Supportive but with significant caveats or preconditions
values.philosophical: Engages at the architectural/paradigm level more than the practical

use_cases.description: Permission Ticket use cases the participant substantively engaged with

values.patient_access: Patient-directed access to their own records across systems
values.payer_claims: Payer requesting clinical data for claims, prior auth, risk adjustment
values.public_health: Public health agency follow-up queries after case reports
values.caregiver_proxy: Caregiver or authorized representative accessing on behalf of patient
values.cds_hooks: CDS Hooks token exchange / clinical decision support integration
values.write_access: Writing data back to remote systems (referral status, task updates)

spec_topics.description: Spec design areas the participant had substantive positions on

values.trust_establishment: How data holders decide which issuers to trust
values.issuer_model: Who issues tickets — data holders, third parties, apps, networks
values.scoping: Granularity of access constraints in the ticket (resource types, conditions, time)
values.consent_representation: How patient consent is encoded/conveyed in or alongside the ticket
values.org_identity: Conveying the requesting organization's identity in SMART flows
values.token_lifetime: Duration of grants, refresh patterns, revocation
values.purpose_of_use: How purpose-of-use is coded and consumed by business rules
values.client_binding: Tying tickets to specific client keys to prevent misuse
values.subject_resolution: Matching ticket subject to local patient identity
values.ds4p: Data Segmentation for Privacy — sensitive data categories
values.format_alignment: Alignment with UDAP, HL7 Consent, Scalable Consent Management
values.discoverability: Machine-readable publication of which issuers a data holder accepts
values.deployment_model: How the spec gets packaged/distributed (containers, open source)
values.category_authorization: Approving classes of requesters rather than individual apps
values.data_holder_issuance: Data holder as ticket issuer (not just consumer)

concerns.description: Risks, failure modes, or barriers the participant raised

values.adoption_barriers: Organizational, cultural, or prioritization barriers to adoption
values.workforce_gap: Staff/developers don't understand FHIR or modern auth well enough
values.vendor_lock_in: Risk of vendors gatekeeping or adding cost layers
values.over_provisioning: Granting broader access than necessary or intended
values.data_segmentation_limits: EHR platforms can't reliably enforce granular data filtering
values.consent_not_verifiable: Responding systems can't verify patient actually consented
values.provider_resistance: Providers refusing to honor credentials or accept partial records
values.issuer_viability: No clear business model for independent consent/identity issuers
values.spec_proliferation: Risk of divergent or duplicative standards
values.scope_governance: Difficulty reaching consensus on purpose-of-use or scope categories
values.security_blast_radius: Compromised credentials enabling broad unauthorized access
values.partial_record_acceptance: Receiving providers unwilling to act on redacted/incomplete data

frameworks_referenced.description: Standards, regulations, or frameworks substantively discussed (not just name-dropped)

key_terms.description: Distinctive concepts, metaphors, or framings introduced by this participant. Free-form — not from a controlled vocabulary. Use lowercase_snake_case.
